title: Tig Fromm
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Tig_Fromm.jpg]] |name=Tig Fromm |homeworld=[[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]]{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Annoo-dat Blue]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{CSWECite|I|297|Fromm, Tig}} |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin=[[Color/Legends|Green]]{{DroidsCite|The White Witch}} |cyber= |families= |parents=[[Sise Fromm]] |partners= |siblings=[[Sonko Fromm]]{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} |children= |affiliation=[[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I am Tig Fromm. I am of the new ways. Technology is the key to the power of the new Fromm Gang, not ancient blood ties and old-world methods. Those are the ways of my father, Sise.|Tig Fromm|}} '''Tig Fromm''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Annoo-dat Blue]] [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] and, as the son of [[Sise Fromm]], heir to the most powerful crime family in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Fromm ran the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang's]] operation on [[Ingo/Legends|Ingo]], where he developed a belief that the Fromm Gang could achieve dominance over their rivals through the acquisition of advanced technology and, to this end, designed and manufactured his own [[Droid/Legends|droids]] to keep [[Tig Fromm's base|his secret base]] functioning and to provide security. However, a series of misadventures with his creations earned him the nicknames '''Tiggy''', '''Baby-Face Fromm''', and '''Junior Fromm'''. When Fromm learned that the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Lonn Idd]] had developed a powerful new [[Laser/Legends|laser]], Fromm attempted to steal it for use by his gang, but was thwarted by the intervention of the droids [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. Fromm later began his most ambitious project: constructing the [[Starship/Legends|weapons satellite]] ''[[Trigon One]]'' as part of a plot to overthrow the Fromm Gang's rivals. Fearing that the secret project had been discovered by the local [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]] racers [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]], Fromm kidnapped Dusat to find out what he knew. Dusat was [[Mission to Ingo|rescued]] by Joben, [[Kea Moll]] of the [[Annoo resistance]], C-3PO and R2-D2, who later returned to Fromm's base and stole the ''Trigon One''. Hoping to recover the vessel, Fromm captured the young rebels and took them to his [[Parent/Legends|father's]] [[Sise Fromm's stronghold|stronghold]] on [[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]], where Joben revealed the ''Trigon One''{{'s}} location. Fromm retrieved the weapons [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] only to discover that Joben had sabotaged it to collide with his father's base. Although both Fromms escaped before the crash, the loss of both the stronghold and the ''Trigon One'' effectively brought about the downfall of the Fromm Gang. Intent on gaining revenge and redeeming himself in his father's [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], Fromm pursued Joben and Dusat to [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]], where he planted a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] aboard Joben's landspeeder, the ''[[White Witch]]'', ahead of the [[Boonta Speeder Race]]. Sise Fromm, however, had hired the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to capture Joben; as Fett chased Joben during the race, the thermal detonator was dislodged and destroyed Fett's own ''[[Silver Speeder]]''. Deciding to cut his losses, Fett captured both Fromms and turned them over to rival [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]. They were later released after paying off the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|Won't my father be proud when he sees what I've done!''"
"''Yes…it just might make up for all the other times you thought he'd be proud of you!|Tig Fromm and Vlix Oncard|[[Droids (1986) 2|''Droids'' (1986) 2]]}} [[File:Tig_Fromm_art.jpg|thumb|left|160px|Tig Fromm]] Born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Annoo (Gelefil)|Annoo]], Tig Fromm was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Annoo-dat Blue]] and the son of the renowned [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] [[Sise Fromm]], leader of the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]]. His brother, Sise's eldest son [[Sonko Fromm]], served as their [[Parent/Legends|father's]] right hand in the organization until he was captured during a failed [[Transport/Legends|transport]] raid and [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] in the [[Prison/Legends|dungeon]] of Sise's [[Sise Fromm's stronghold|stronghold]] on Annoo as punishment. Tig too followed in his father's footsteps, becoming a gangster of some repute.''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{C|"Tig Fromm"}} He became increasingly convinced that his father's methods and reliance on muscle power were outdated and that technology represented the future of the Fromm Gang. Establishing a [[Tig Fromm's base|secret base]] in the [[Vaj Desert]] on [[Ingo/Legends|Ingo]], Fromm began designing and manufacturing his own [[Droid/Legends|droids]] at the base, including the [[Fromm Tower Droid]],''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' and made use of his creations to keep the base functioning and to provide security. Though he was given the freedom to run his operation largely independently from his father's, the two would often work in cooperation. However, Fromm's reliance on technology was known to backfire. On one occasion, droids assigned to protect his father malfunctioned, turning on Sise and handing him over to the authorities. Fromm's reputation was further degraded when the attempted rescue of his father resulted in him breaking somebody else out and leaving Sise there.{{DroidsCite|Escape Into Terror}} On another occasion, Fromm accidentally destroyed his father's vacation palace on [[Bolad]]. Fromm's failures earned him the nicknames Baby-Face Fromm and Fromm Junior. Sise, embarrassed with his son, assigned [[Vlix Oncard]] to act as Tig's personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] and keep watch over him. Oncard never let Fromm forget his failures, frequently referring to him by the derogatory nickname Tiggy. ===The ultimate weapon=== {{Quote|The laser's installed, Fromm…now leave me and my family in peace!''"
"''Sure, I'll leave you, Idd! Not in peace!!…but in pieces!!|Lonn Idd and Tig Fromm|}} Fromm's interest in technology led him to search for increasingly powerful weapons. Upon learning that the [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Lonn Idd]] had developed a powerful new [[Laser/Legends|laser]] to aid in his droid research, Fromm set off for [[Lonn Idd's repair station|Idd's repair station]] to capture the scientist and demand that he install the device on [[Tig Fromm's starship|Tig's ship]]. Accompanied by Oncard, a pair of Annoo-dat Blue guards and several [[hover guard]] and [[Interrogation droid/Legends|interrogation droids]], Fromm hoped to use the threat of force to persuade Idd to do as he asked. [[File:TigFromm.png|thumb|right|230px|Tig Fromm menaces the Idd family.]] Aboard the [[Space station/Legends|space station]], Fromm was about to begin [[Torture/Legends|physical persuasion]] when Lonn's children, [[Vik Idd|Vik]] and [[Nikki Idd|Nikki]], returned to the station on their [[Intergalactic Droid Agency shuttle|shuttle]] along with the droids [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. Nikki walked in on her father's interrogation, providing Fromm with a new means of persuasion: threatening to hurt Nikki if Idd didn't cooperate. Before Nikki was captured, however, she was able to activate the station's communications system, alerting her brother to what was happening. R2-D2 suggested a plan to rescue Nikki where C-3PO would cause his [[Counterpart/Legends|counterpart]] to blow a fuse, distracting the guards, while Vik rescued his sister. The plan failed, however, due to the intervention of a hover guard, and both Idd children were now in Fromm's custody. Lonn Idd reluctantly completed the installation of the laser, but Fromm planned to test its power by destroying the station as he left, [[Death/Legends|killing]] the inhabitants. Just before he could leave, C-3PO arrived wearing the empty shell of a [[HK Guardian Droid|guardian droid]]. Fromm, mistaking it for a real guardian droid, planned to take the valuable droid with him and demanded its [[control disk]]. Instead, Nikki Idd threw [[Perfume/Legends|perfume]] in Fromm's face, momentarily blinding him. C-3PO took the opportunity to attack and, protected by his [[Armor/Legends|armor]], proved virtually invulnerable to the weapons of Fromm's hover guards. As C-3PO backed the Annoo-dat Blue into a corner, Vik activated a control, dropping a [[packing net]] on them and trapping the gangsters. Lonn Idd subsequently turned Fromm and his companions over to the authorities. Mindful of his brother's fate, Fromm avoided contacting his father for some time following his release from prison. ===Power play=== {{Quote|Be careful, Mister Dusat. I am an ambitious man…ambitious men can be dangerous.|Tig Fromm threatens Jord Dusat|}} By [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],"[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]" in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]'' states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of '' A New Hope'' in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], meaning that ''Star Wars: Droids'' takes place in [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]]. Fromm and Oncard were free to pursue a new scheme for power. Despite Sise's distaste for technology, Fromm persuaded his father to invest heavily in a covert project to build a powerful [[Starship/Legends|weapons satellite]] dubbed the ''[[Trigon One]]'' and designed by Fromm himself. Despite its relatively small size, Fromm assured his father that it would have the firepower of something a thousand times its size. Using its [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] laser, the Fromms planned to move against other gangs and secure their place as the dominant criminal organization in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Placed in charge of the project, Fromm oversaw the construction at his secret base. As the ''Trigon One'' neared completion, word of its development reached [[Demma Moll]], leader of the [[Annoo resistance]], who [[Mission to Ingo|dispatched]] her daughter, [[Kea Moll|Kea]], to investigate. Upon arriving, the young freedom fighter saw Fromm's old enemies, the droids R2-D2 and C-3PO, together with their new owners, the [[Landspeeder/Legends|speeder]] racers [[Thall Joben/Legends|Thall Joben]] and [[Jord Dusat]], accidentally enter the restricted zone around Fromm's base. With Moll's help the group was able to escape from the [[Sentry droid/Legends|sentry droids]] which had moved to intercept the intruders. [[File:TigFromm4.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Tig Fromm in his office on Ingo]] Fromm and Oncard feared that the location of the base might reach the other gang leaders and compromise the ''Trigon One'' project. Deciding that the speeder racers needed to be silenced before they could talk, Fromm led a group of [[muscle droid]]s to their [[Ingo speeder shop|speeder shop]] to capture them. Arriving while Joben and the droids were away, the droids ensnared Dusat and waited for Joben's return. However, Moll intervened once more to help Joben escape the trap. Fromm had Dusat taken back to his base, where he and Oncard interrogated the prisoner to discover how much he and Joben knew. Before Fromm could have Dusat transferred to his father's stronghold on Annoo, however, Joben, Moll, and the droids were able to break into the base in a rescue attempt. When Fromm became aware of the situation, he dispatched [[Snic 2-4-2|Strong Arm Droids]] to ambush Joben, Dusat, and R2-D2 in their speeder, the ''[[White Witch]]'', as they made their way out of the base. However, C-3PO and Kea Moll had gained access to the security system and redirected them to a different area to allow their allies to pass. Frustrated, Fromm tried again to stop their egress, ordering his [[droid cruiser]]s outside to await them and disabling his sentry droids to prevent them attacking the cruisers. However, C-3PO was again able to use the security system, this [[Time/Legends|time]] to reactivate the sentry droids. As the two groups of droids opened fire on each other, the ''White Witch'' escaped. ===Loss of the ''Trigon One''=== {{Quote|You can't put a price on an entire star system. Soon this whole quadrant will be ours, to do with as we please.|Tig Fromm, to Sise Fromm|}} [[File:TigFromm3.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Tig Fromm shows the finished ''Trigon One'' to his father.]] In the aftermath of Joben's rescue, Fromm stepped up production of his Fromm Tower Droids to replace those destroyed in battle against the cruisers. The young gangster feared that the racers' escape would lead to the other gangs finding out about the ''Trigon One'' but, as luck would have it, as the group traveled to [[Boonta/Legends|Boonta]] for the [[Boonta Speeder Race]], a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] malfunction caused them to head to Annoo for repairs. Fromm contacted his father to apologize for his failure, only to learn that the Fromm Gang's [[Spy/Legends|spies]] had already reported the group's presence on Annoo to Sise, who vowed to deal with them himself. {{Unlicensedstart}} At some point, Fromm moved the ''Trigon One'' into hiding in the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] while he prepared for its launch. When Joben and Dusat intercepted a message from Fromm to his father, they located the weapons satellite and set out to prevent its launch. While Joben created a distraction, Dusat and R2-D2 boarded the ship and disabled it before the group made their escape, leaving Sise once more furious with his son.{{StoryCite|story=Neutralizing Trigon I|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 1|sformatted=Neutralizing ''Trigon I''}} {{Unlicensedend}} With the ''Trigon One'' nearing completion, Fromm traveled to Annoo to pick up his father for an inspection of the base on Ingo. By the time he arrived, the young racers had yet again escaped an attack by the Fromm Gang and had learned the whole truth about what the Fromms were planning from Demma Moll. Upon hearing that Fromm had come to Annoo, Joben, Moll and the droids found a way to infiltrate [[Sise Fromm's starship|his father's starship]] for the journey to Ingo, in the hopes of gaining access to Fromm's base and destroying the ''Trigon One''. With the [[Human/Legends|Humans]] hidden in a pair of cargo containers supposedly carrying parts for the ''Trigon One'', the infiltrators were able to gain access to the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where the final work was being completed on the weapons [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]]. Observing from a control room, Fromm quickly recognized the intruders and ordered his forces to stop them, but they were able to steal the ''Trigon One'' and escape. Believing that the ''Trigon One''{{'s}} weapons systems were still inactive, Fromm dispatched [[Droid fighter (Fromm Gang)|droid starfighters]] to intercept. However, R2-D2 was able to activate the weapons systems and the [[Starfighter/Legends|fighters]] were destroyed as the ''Trigon One'' fled Annoo. With the loss of the ''Trigon One'', the future of the Fromm Gang seemed bleak. Knowing that the other gangs would unite against them, Fromm suggested starting again somewhere else and building a replacement for the ''Trigon One''. However, his father—blaming Fromm for what had happened—insisted that he would operate without his son in future. {{Unlicensedstart}} In retaliation for the loss of the ''Trigon One'', Sise's agents captured Kea Moll and imprisoned her in Sise's heaquarters, demanding that Joben and Dusat return the weapons satellite in exchange for her release. However, R2-D2 and C-3PO infiltrated the facility and freed Moll from her cell. As the trio fled, Tig ordered his guards to intercept, but they were unable to prevent their escape.{{StoryCite|story=Kea Kidnapped|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 2}} {{Unlicensedend}} ===The fall of the Fromm Gang=== {{Quote|So you see, it doesn't help anyone to have all these gangs around. I mean half the time you don't know who's victimizing who.|Tig Fromm, to Thall Joben|{{DroidsCite|The Trigon Unleashed}}}} [[File:TigFromm5.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Tig Fromm on the bridge of the ''Trigon One'']] Desperate to prove himself to his father, Fromm planned to capture Joben, Moll and the droids and learn where they had hidden the ''Trigon One''. To this end, he sent Oncard with a group of his Annoo-dat guards to attack them at the speeder shop on Ingo. Though they escaped in a landspeeder, they were pursued and channeled into a trap. Oncard returned the prisoners to the speeder shop, where Fromm personally interrogated them. Joben initially refused to tell Fromm where he had taken the ''Trigon One'' but, upon learning that Dusat and Demma have been captured by Sise's gang on Annoo, he agreed to reveal the location, but only to Sise himself. Angry at yet another humiliation, Fromm departed for Annoo with his captives aboard a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]]. After arriving at Sise's stronghold, the prisoners were taken before both Fromms while Sise questioned Joben, during which the young racer revealed that the ''Trigon One'' was hidden in the [[Jarl forest]] on Ingo. Fromm asked his father to let him personally retrieve the ship, to which his father agreed, but only if Oncard accompany him. Returning to Ingo, Fromm recovered the ''Trigon One'' and flew the vessel back to Annoo, leaving Oncard to return in the [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]]. On the way back, he contacted his father to demonstrate the capabilities of the primary weapon to Sise, impressing the elder Fromm by destroying an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]]. However, Sise's newfound respect for his son was not to last. Joben and R2-D2 had sabotaged the ''Trigon One'' and, as Fromm attempted to engage the docking sequence, it entered a collision course for Sise's stronghold. After trying in vain to alter the ship's course, Fromm was forced to flee aboard Oncard's shuttle, from which he contacted his father once more to warn him to evacuate the base. Sise escaped, as did the young freedom fighters, moments before the ''Trigon One'' collided with the stronghold, turning it into a crater. The loss of the base on Annoo, together with the weapons satellite, effectively spelled the end of the Fromm Gang. Sise was furious with his son. News of the Fromms' plan soon reached rival [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]. The quick-witted [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] struck while the iron was hot and placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on the heads of the entire Fromm organization, aiming to crush them while they were still reeling from their losses.{{DroidsCite|A Race to the Finish}} ===Revenge=== {{Quote|Your meddling has cost me my droid, my speeder and almost my neck, Tig Fromm.|Boba Fett, to Tig Fromm|}} Intent on getting revenge, the Fromms pursued the young rebels in Tig's modified ''Lambda''-class shuttle, the ''[[Voor Viper]]'', as they resumed their course for Boonta. As Moll's [[Starrunner-class starship|''Starrunner''-class starship]], the ''[[Sand Sloth]]'', prepared to make the jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], Fromm opened fire and succeeded in damaging the other ship's [[Engine/Legends|engines]] and injuring Dusat. However, a weapons malfunction on the rundown shuttle allowed the speeder racers to escape before he could destroy the ''Sand Sloth''. Despite having their own engine problems, the Fromms entered hyperspace to pursue their adversaries to Boonta. After arriving on Boonta, Sise contacted the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and asked him to capture the speeder racers and their companions. Since Fett owed Sise a favor, he agreed to overlook Jabba's bounty and do as the elder Fromm asked. Tig, however, was determined to prove himself to his father and resolved to beat the bounty hunter to the punch and get revenge. After enlisting Oncard's help—in exchange for the promise of a new wardrobe—the pair located the garage where the racers were storing the ''White Witch'' ahead of the upcoming Boonta Speeder Race. Fromm planned to plant a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]] on the ''White Witch'', but arrived just as a gas leak in the garage caused R2-D2 to break down the door in order to get Moll outside to safety. [[File:FrommsGetBusted-ARttF.png|thumb|right|250px|Boba Fett apprehends the Fromm Gang.]] Taking cover nearby, Fromm and Oncard followed C-3PO as the droid moved the ''White Witch'' to another facility. There, Boba Fett attacked Joben and Moll, providing Fromm with the cover he needed to plant the thermal detonator on the ''White Witch'', setting it to detonate at the end of the Boonta Speeder Race's tenth and final lap. Thall Joben escaped in the ''White Witch'' and headed straight for the Boonta Speeder Race with Fett in pursuit. However, [[Proto One]], a droid the racers had met on Boonta, saw Fromm plant the device and warned Moll. Fromm headed straight to the Boonta Speeder Race, where his family had requisitioned a private viewing box from its unwitting owners. By the time Moll reached the race track, the ''White Witch'' was already participating in the race. With no way for Joben's friends to warn him, it appeared that Fromm's plan was set to work. However, Fett was pursuing Joben in his ''[[Silver Speeder]]''. Fett attacked Joben throughout the race, unaware of the thermal detonator. An attack involving [[Magnet/Legends|magnets]] accidentally loosened the detonator, which attached itself to the ''Silver Speeder''. Although Fett [[Ejector seat/Legends|ejected]] in time, the ''Silver Speeder'' was destroyed. Joben went on to win the race. After the race, the Fromms confronted the victorious racer and his companions one last time. Interrupted by a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]-wielding Proto One, they attempted to flee but ran straight into Boba Fett. Fett blamed Fromm's meddling for the loss of his speeder and almost his life. Capturing Fromm, as well as his father and Oncard, Fett took them to Jabba to claim the reward. The trio's imprisonment proved short-lived, however; the Fromm's bribed Jabba into canceling the bounty on them by agreeing to pay him a percentage of their profits and committing their Annoo-dat gangsters to eliminating the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]].{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} {{Unlicensedstart}} At some point, Fromm dispatched Oncard to intercept Moll, C-3PO and R2-D2 on Annoo in an attempt to retrieve a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] in Moll's possession which contained instructions on how to build a [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear missile]]. Oncard brought the trio before Fromm and he agreed to let them go after Moll turned the datacard over to him, unaware that C-3PO had wiped the data it contained.{{StoryCite|story=The Secret Disk|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 6}} Fromm later invented a method of producing an artificial smoke cloud in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] which would allow him to prey on passing vessels. While testing the device, Fromm ensnared C-3PO and R2-D2's transport, but the two droids boarded Fromm's ship and deactivated the device, allowing them to escape.{{StoryCite|story=The Cloud|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 7}} {{Unlicensedend}} ==Personality and traits== [[File:Tig_and_family.jpg|thumb|left|280px|The leaders of the Fromm Gang with Boba Fett]] {{Quote|If it's not a droid, I don't trust it…and I'm not my father, I'm me.|Tig Fromm|}} As the son of Sise Fromm, Tig spent much of his life in his father's shadow and, despite running the Fromm Gang's operations on Ingo, his reputation failed to reach the level of his father's. Seeking a way to differentiate himself from what he saw as Sise's antiquated methods, the young Fromm developed a fascination with technology, and over time, an aptitude for designing droids and other advanced technology which he believed were superior to [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] due to greater loyalty and power. Such was the level of Fromm's knowledge of droids that, in addition to [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] and [[Dat-an]], he was also able to understand their [[Binary/Legends|Binary]] language. However, Fromm ultimately became over-dependent on technology, and his plans often failed due to malfunctions in his droids. Although Fromm always had an excuse ready when something went wrong, his frequent failures left Sise with little faith in his abilities, and the elder Fromm assigned Vlix Oncard to watch over him, for which Fromm long resented his father.[[Droids (picture book)|''Droids'' Spanish picture book]] In addition to his father's own contempt of Fromm's abilities and threats of what would happen if he failed again, Oncard's frequent mocking of Fromm and use of the nickname Tiggy proved a source of great frustration, and Fromm relished the opportunity to take his revenge on Oncard whenever possible. Fromm was determined to finally prove his worth to his father but, despite frequent suggestions from Oncard that he use gang members for jobs, he stubbornly refused, becoming increasingly reliant on droids and distrustful of everybody else. At the same time, Fromm was aware of his father's advanced age and looked forward to the day when he could take charge of the whole Fromm gang and put Oncard in his place. Fromm was ambitious and had plans to increase the Fromm Gang's power and secure their place among the galaxy's most powerful gangs. He was ruthless in pursuing his goals and willing to torture or kill anybody who stood in his way, including Lonn Idd's children. When confronted by Boba Fett over the loss of the ''Silver Speeder'', Fromm quickly turned on Oncard, attempting to blame his bodyguard for what went wrong. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:DroidsStarlog.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Promotional image for ''Star Wars: Droids'' with a possible early design for Tig Fromm]] Tig Fromm was created for the animated series ''[[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'', in which he first appeared in the episode "[[The White Witch]]," written by [[Peter Sauder]] and first broadcast on [[September 7]], [[1985]].{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}} Fromm served as the main antagonist throughout the first story arc, consisting of the episodes "The White Witch," "[[Escape Into Terror]]," "[[The Trigon Unleashed]]," and "[[A Race to the Finish]]." Fromm also appeared in a series of [[Wikipedia:Picture book|picture book]] adaptations of the ''Droids'' episodes published by [[Dragon Picture Books]] in [[1987]][[The White Witch (book)|''The White Witch'' picture book]][[Escape Into Terror (book)|''Escape Into Terror'' picture book]][[The Trigon Unleashed (book)|''The Trigon Unleashed'' picture book]][[A Race to the Finish (book)|''A Race to the Finish'' picture book]] and ''[[The White Witch: A Droid Adventure]]'', a [[1986]] adaptation of the series' opening episode, written by [[Emily James]] and published by [[Random House]].''[[The White Witch: A Droid Adventure]]'' The character later appeared in [[Droids (1986) 2|''Droids'' (1986) 2]], the second issue of [[Marvel Comics|Marvel's]] [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] comic series, written by [[David Manak]] and illustrated by [[John Romita Sr.]] The comic's position in the timeline is unclear, but the fact that the Fromm Gang is still around places it before the events depicted in the animated series. Both of Fromm's appearances hinted at his many earlier failures, and these were later elaborated on in the [[2004]] [[Rich Handley]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] article ''[[Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'', which appeared in ''[[Polyhedron 170]]'' and was illustrated by [[Jeffery Carlisle|Jeff Carlisle]]. Among Carlisle's inspirations for the article's illustrations was an early promotional image for ''Star Wars: Droids'' which appeared in [[Wikipedia:Starlog|''Starlog'']] magazine and which what Carlisle has speculated may have been a female version of Fromm.{{SWArchive|url=vault/collecting/jeff_carlisle_droids_revisited/index.html|text=''Droids'' Revisited: An Interview with Artist Jeff Carlisle}} Fromm has also been mentioned in several reference works, including entries in ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' ([[1994]]), the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[1998]]), ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' ([[2000]]), and ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[2008]]).''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"Fromm, Tig"}}''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' {{C|"Fromm, Tig"}} A Tig Fromm action figure was produced by [[Kenner Products|Kenner]] as part of its [[Star Wars: Droids (toy line)|''Star Wars: Droids'']] collection to accompany the television series.{{SWArchive|url=vault/collecting/droids_ewoks_25th_anniversary/index.html|text=25 Years of Collecting ''Droids'' and ''Ewoks''!}} In 1986, [[Wikipedia:Spain|Spanish]] publisher [[Editorial Gepsa]] released a [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]] called [[MyComyc]] featuring characters from popular cartoon series, including ''Star Wars: Droids'' and [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']], in a number of two-page short adventures. The comics were unknown in the [[Wikipedia:United States|United States]] until [[2013]], when [[Dark Horse Comics]] Vice President of Publishing [[Randy Stradley]] explored the possibility of republishing them. However, [[Lucasfilm]] staff were unable to find proof that the comics were officially licensed and they are considered ambiguously [[canon]].{{SW|url=news/droids-and-ewoks-return-spains-lost-star-wars-comic-strips|text=Droids and Ewoks Return: Spain's Lost ''Star Wars'' Comic Strips|int=Droids and Ewoks Return: Spain's Lost Star Wars Comic Strips|nolive=1}} Fromm was featured in the MyComyc stories ''[[Neutralizing Trigon I]]'', ''[[Kea Kidnapped]]'', ''[[The Secret Disk]]'', and ''[[The Cloud]]''. {{StoryCite|story=The Stolen Ship|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 3}} also depicts Oncard talking via [[Subspace transceiver/Legends|subspace transceiver]] to "the boss," but it is unclear whether this is intended to be Tig or Sise Fromm.{{StoryCite|story=The Stolen Ship|book=MyComyc|bformatted=''MyComyc'' 3}} The ''MyComyc'' stories depict events which are not shown in the television series and their chronological placement is unclear. Abel G. Peña, who translated the comics to English, has commented that ''Neutralizing Trigon I'' and ''Kea Kidnapped'' may be an alternate retelling of events in the ''Droids'' cartoon.{{JCFCite|url=posts/50635555|board=Literature|thread=Obscure Droids and Ewoks comics -- translated by Abel Peña|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2013-04-11|time=1:15 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20150921222207/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/obscure-droids-and-ewoks-comics-translated-by-abel-pe%C3%B1a.50010980/page-2#post-50635555}} [[Droids (picture book)|''Droids'']], a Spanish picture-book adaptation of "The White Witch," contains some differences to the episode. In this version, Joben arrives in the ''White Witch'' while Fromm and Oncard are interrogating Dusat, knocking down a wall and rescuing his friend while the Annoo-dat gangsters run for cover in another room and sound the alarm. After Dusat is rescued from Fromm's base, his father takes personal control of the ''Trigon One'' project, ordering Fromm to transfer all material to another base and destroy the Ingo facility to keep it from falling into enemy hands. Abel G. Peña, who translated ''Droids'' into English in [[2014]], speculated that such differences may be due to the novelization being based on an early script rather than the final episode.{{SW|url=news/of-androids-sidereal-beings-and-gourd-heads-spains-droids-and-ewoks-novelizations|series=Spain's Droids and Ewoks Novelizations|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} In ''Droids'', the Fromm Gang is said to have "innumerable droids and androids (and, above all, clones)" in its service while both Fromm and Oncard are described as "[[Android/Legends|androids]] half-human and half-reptiloid in appearance." Peña has suggested that this may suggest that Fromm and Oncard are [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]].{{SW|url=news/of-androids-sidereal-beings-and-gourd-heads-spains-droids-and-ewoks-novelizations-part-2|series=Spain's Droids and Ewoks Novelizations|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}
